questions.question: Isolation Forest labels -1 for:
questions.explanation: IsolationForest.predict() returns -1 for anomalies and 1 for normal points.

questions.question: Z-score outlier detection typically flags points beyond:
questions.explanation: 3 standard deviations captures 99.7% of normally distributed data. Points beyond are flagged as outliers.

questions.question: LOF detects anomalies by comparing:
options: Local density to that of k nearest neighbours
questions.explanation: LOF measures how isolated a point is relative to its local neighbourhood density.

questions.question: The main challenge with real-world anomaly detection is:
questions.explanation: In fraud or fault detection, anomalies may be <1% of data, making standard accuracy metrics meaningless.

questions.question: An Autoencoder detects anomalies because:
options: High reconstruction error indicates the model cannot recreate unusual inputs
questions.explanation: Autoencoders trained on normal data struggle to reconstruct anomalies, resulting in high reconstruction error.

questions.question: The 'contamination' parameter in IsolationForest sets:
options: The expected fraction of anomalies in the dataset
questions.explanation: contamination tells the algorithm what fraction of samples to label as anomalies when calling predict().

questions.question: One-Class SVM is useful when:
options: Both normal and anomaly labels are available
Only normal data is available during training
questions.explanation: One-Class SVM learns a boundary around normal data without any anomaly examples.

questions.question: ROC-AUC is preferred for anomaly detection evaluation because:
options: It is threshold-independent and works well with imbalanced classes
questions.explanation: ROC-AUC summarises performance across all classification thresholds, important when the optimal threshold is unknown.

questions.question: DBSCAN can be used for anomaly detection because:
options: Points in sparse regions are labelled as noise (-1)
It always finds the right number of clusters
questions.explanation: DBSCAN's noise points (label -1) are naturally isolated from dense clusters — effectively anomaly candidates.

questions.question: Which metric should NOT be used as the only metric for imbalanced anomaly detection?
questions.explanation: With 99% normal data, a model that always predicts 'normal' gets 99% accuracy — completely useless.
